# Project Management Plan (PMP)  


## Purpose of the PMP

This Project Management Plan (PMP) defines **how the project will be executed,
monitored, controlled, and closed**, in alignment with:

- Approved Project Charter
- Strategy & Governance (Value Stream, OKRs, KPIs)
- Business Case
- Scope Baseline (Scope Statement, WBS, WBS Dictionary)
- Stakeholder Register
- Requirements Specification

The PMP is the **authoritative execution reference** for the project.

---

## Project Governance Overview

### Governance Model

- **Ecology-first governance**: ecological KPIs take precedence
- **Stage-Gate decision model** based on KPI thresholds
- **Human-in-the-loop** mandatory for all ecological decisions

### Decision Authorities

| Decision Type | Authority |
|--------------|-----------|
| Strategic Direction | Sponsor (NOAA / SUB) |
| Ecological Acceptance | Environmental SME Lead |
| Technical Decisions | Data Science Lead |
| Delivery & Control | Project Manager |

---

## Scope Management Plan

- Scope is defined by the **Scope Baseline**
- No work outside approved WBS is permitted
- Scope changes require:
  - Formal Change Request
  - Impact analysis (ecological, schedule, cost)
  - Sponsor approval

---

## Schedule Management Plan

- Schedule derived directly from WBS
- Milestone-driven planning
- Stage-Gates act as **hard control points**
- Ecological validation tasks are non-compressible

---

## Cost Management Plan

- Cost categories:
  - Personnel (Data Science, Engineering, SMEs)
  - Compute & storage
  - Governance & validation effort
- Contingency reserves maintained for annotation uncertainty
- Cost performance reviewed at Stage-Gates

---

## Quality Management Plan (Integrated)

### Quality Objectives
- Protect benthic-relevant data
- Maintain ecological confidence
- Ensure reproducibility and auditability

### Quality Control
- KPI-3 (Ecological Data Protection Index) is mandatory
- SME validation is required for acceptance
- Documentation of limitations is compulsory

---

## Resource Management Plan

| Role | Responsibility |
|-----|----------------|
| Project Manager | Coordination and control |
| Product Owner | OKR ownership |
| Environmental SMEs | Ecological validation |
| Data Science Team | Model development |
| Data Engineering Team | Pipeline & assets |

---

## Communication Management Plan

| Stakeholder | Information | Frequency |
|------------|-------------|-----------|
| Sponsor | Status & risks | Monthly / Gates |
| SMEs | Validation results | Weekly |
| PO & PM | Delivery alignment | ≥ 2× per week |
| Team | Execution updates | Weekly |

---

## Risk Management Plan

- Risk Register maintained and reviewed continuously
- Ecological risks prioritized
- Any degradation of KPI-3 triggers escalation

---

## Change Management Plan

- Formal Change Control Log maintained
- All changes assessed for ecological impact
- Unauthorized scope changes prohibited

---

## Procurement Management Plan

- Only license-compliant AI frameworks
- Internal infrastructure preferred
- External procurement subject to Petrobras rules

---

## Stakeholder Engagement Plan

- Engagement strategies derived from Stakeholder Register
- Environmental SMEs engaged continuously
- Sponsor involved at all Stage-Gates

---

## Integration with OKRs, KPIs and Stage-Gates

- OKR 2.1 defines value
- KPIs define measurement
- Stage-Gates define control
- PMP defines execution

---

## Baseline Management

Baselines maintained:
- Scope Baseline
- Schedule Baseline (to be approved)
- Cost Baseline (to be approved)

Changes to baselines require Sponsor approval.

---

## Project Closure Criteria

- MVP ecological acceptance achieved
- KPIs validated
- Assets versioned and stored
- Lessons learned documented
- Sponsor sign-off
